title: Specify discovery lead
description: Validates a single raw, unmerged `Lead` block under `## Lead inventory` in `discovery.md` per workflow §Discovery handshake. Each block is one lead as surfaced by one source: a `source` naming the binding that produced it, a kebab-case `lead` (unique only within that `source`), a content-bearing per-source `synopsis`, and an optional `aliases[]` list of kebab-case aliases. Identity is the `(source, lead)` pair; the same `lead` MAY appear under different source keys, and cross-source unification is deferred to `/spec:plan`'s plan-time `propose` sub-step. `slices[].sources[].lead` resolves first against `lead`, then against any `aliases[]` entry, within the binding's `source`; aliases are stable across re-survey. An alias MUST NOT collide with another lead's `lead` or `aliases[]` under the same `source` (`discovery-alias-collision` from `specrun slice validate`).

lead.description: Stable kebab-case lead identifier, unique only within its `source`. Re-survey of that source replaces the block by `(source, lead)`.

source.description: Source binding key that surfaced this lead (matches a top-level `plan.yaml.sources.<key>` binding). A `survey` attributes every lead it produces to its own source key.

synopsis.description: Reconciliation-grade per-source headline of the lead as this source surfaced it. SHOULD name the operation/surface and its salient constraint distinctly enough that a same-slug lead from another source can be matched or distinguished on content, not just the slug. MAY span more than one line when one is too thin. Plan-time headline material only — never a back-door for slice-time `Evidence`, which remains the job of `extract`.

aliases.description: Optional kebab-case aliases for cross-source unification. `slices[].sources[].lead` resolves a binding's lead against the lead's `lead` or any `alias` under the same `source`. Authored by the source adapter at `survey` time, or added by the operator through `specrun plan amend --add-alias`; `/spec:plan` never hand-edits `discovery.md`. Stable across re-survey; operator additions survive re-survey. Empty `aliases: []` and a missing field are equivalent.
